compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Root cause proven (illegal `runner` context in job-level env of reconcile-bridge.yml:76, added by f2bde8d3b5 -> parse-time startup failure -> 0 jobs -> no Jira sync for ~2 days). Verified with actionlint RED->GREEN + mutation check. Sibling in reconcile-bridge-canary.yml:69 fixed under same discipline; full workflow re-sweep clean. Change up for review: Gerrit 734 (https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/734). Awaiting LLM-Review +1 and Verified +1, then Submit; will confirm the reconcile-bridge job creates jobs and turns green on origin/main.

compiled_state.description:
## Summary
The `reconcile-bridge` GitHub Actions workflow (and its `reconcile-bridge-canary`) fail
at parse time on **every** run since commit `f2bde8d3b5` (2026-07-14 16:11 UTC). The
runs complete with conclusion=failure but **zero jobs / zero check-runs** — a GitHub
Actions *startup failure*. Because this workflow is the only mechanism that syncs the
`tickets` branch to Jira, the reconciler has not executed for ~2 days, so **Jira has
diverged from the rebar store** (the reported symptom).

## Reproduction Steps
1. `actionlint .github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml`
2. Observe: `reconcile-bridge.yml:76:39: context "runner" is not allowed here.`
3. Equivalently, every push-triggered run of the workflow returns conclusion=failure with
   `total_count: 0` jobs (`gh api repos/navapbc/rebar/actions/runs/<id>/jobs`).

**Expected:** the workflow parses, the `reconcile` job runs, and rebar↔Jira sync completes.
**Actual:** GitHub rejects the workflow at parse time; no job is created; no sync happens.

## Root Cause
Commit `f2bde8d3b5` added `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY: ${{ runner.temp }}/rebar-bot-signing-key`
to the **job-level `env:`** block. The `runner` context is unavailable outside steps
(available job-env contexts: github, inputs, matrix, needs, secrets, strategy, vars), so
the expression is a hard parse error → startup failure → 0 jobs. The identical line exists
in `reconcile-bridge-canary.yml:69`. The job-level line was also redundant: the
"Materialize the Rebar Bot signing key" step already exports the same path via `$GITHUB_ENV`
before both rebar-write steps (Run reconciler, Commit-back), which run after it.

## Fix
Remove the illegal job-level `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY` line from both workflows (replaced
with a comment documenting the constraint). This restores the last-known-good behavior; the
Materialize step's `$GITHUB_ENV` export already delivers the signing-key path to every
downstream write step. Branch: `fix/reconcile-runner-context`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `actionlint .github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml` exits 0 (no `runner`-context error).
- [ ] `actionlint .github/workflows/reconcile-bridge-canary.yml` exits 0.
- [ ] No context-availability violation remains in any `.github/workflows/*.yml`.
- [ ] [operator-attested] a post-merge `reconcile-bridge` run creates a real job (jobs > 0) and the reconciler executes.
compiled_state.title: reconcile-bridge CI startup failure: illegal runner context in job-level env blocks all Jira sync
